defs: That with which one walks; a foot.
quotes: Lame Mulciber, his walkers quite misgrown.

defs: A forest officer appointed to walk over a certain space for inspection; a forester.

quotes: She cursed the weaver and the walker The cloth that had wrought.

defs: Any ambulatorial orthopterous insect, as a stick insect.

A shift superintendent or assistant superintendent who supervises several shifters{4} and their crews. Since these crews are usually separated by some distance, the walker is frequently seen walking between them. Also called walking boss.
A low frame having casters or wheels, designed to enclose a baby on its sides and provide support while the baby is learning to walk; it usually has a seat so that the baby can sit while not walking.

A low rectangular frame of light metal about waist high, usually having two horizontal arms mounted on four legs with an open rear, designed to be carried or pushed in front of a person whose legs are impaired, to provide support while walking; it may have wheels on two of the four legs.
